hypothesis: If language models for materials design are fine-tuned using Direct Preference Optimization guided by physics-based thermodynamic simulation feedback, then they will generate BCC/B2 superalloy compositions with higher reward scores, greater novelty, and better coverage of the valid design space compared to supervised fine-tuning alone, few-shot prompting of large API models, and traditional generative baselines, because iterative preference learning steers the model's distribution toward physically valid compositions while preserving the diversity learned during supervised fine-tuning.
